2017-09-25.abstract: Bashar al-Assad has not declared victory, but no one is left who could oust him, and regional powers increasingly operate as though he is here to stay.

2017-09-26.title: After Mexico Earthquake, Villagers Are ‘Left With Nothing’
2017-09-26.abstract: With hundreds of houses destroyed and President Trump vowing to limit Mexican immigration, a rural village whose workers flocked to the U.S. wonders how it will rebuild.

2017-09-26.title: Collapsed School in Mexico Earthquake Is Checked for ‘Hidden Defects’
2017-09-26.abstract: The building, constructed before 1985, may never have been upgraded to meet strict new building codes enacted in the wake of that year’s horrific quake.

2017-09-26.title: Palestinian Gunman Kills 3 Israelis at West Bank Crossing
2017-09-26.abstract: The killings shattered the routine at an ordinarily peaceful crossing point. The assailant, a Palestinian father of four, was fatally shot.

2017-09-26.title: Snapshots Along the River Where China Meets North Korea
2017-09-26.abstract: As tensions grow over North Korea’s nuclear tests and China’s sanctions, tourists and residents in the region seemed divided over where to cast blame.

2017-09-26.title: Where It’s Made: Visit a Lego Factory
2017-09-26.abstract: Step inside a factory that makes the bricks for your favorite Lego toys in this 360° tour.

2017-09-27.title: How Couples Are Coping With Trump’s Travel Ban
2017-09-27.abstract: President Trump issued a new order indefinitely barring almost all travel to the United States from seven countries, citing national security threats. We spoke with Iranian and American fiancés separated by the ban.

2017-09-27.title: Is Germany Still a Haven for Israelis? After Election, Some Wonder
2017-09-27.abstract: Some Jews read the worst into gains made by the far-right Alternative for Germany party. Others said Germany was still more progressive than Israel.

2017-09-27.title: As China’s Economy Slows, ‘Business Cults’ Prey on Young Job Seekers
2017-09-27.abstract: Swindlers are seizing on the anxieties of a generation of young Chinese willing to go to great lengths to avoid the shame of unemployment.

2017-09-27.title: Still More Victims in Mexico’s Quakes: Legions of Displaced
2017-09-27.abstract: More than 153,000 homes were damaged in two earthquakes this month, and the future for many who can’t return to them is highly uncertain.

2017-09-27.title: Leaving a Condemned Mexico City Building
2017-09-27.abstract: Since the Sept. 19 earthquake in Mexico, about 500 buildings across Mexico City have been found to be structurally unsafe. At this building on Iturbide street, residents are packing up and leaving.

2017-09-28.title: South Korea Says It’s Speeding Up Arms Buildup to Counter the North
2017-09-28.abstract: President Moon Jae-in also renewed his call for the South’s military to become more independent of America’s, though he said deterring the North was the top priority.

2017-09-28.title: In Paris, Worn-Out Notre-Dame Needs a Makeover, and Hopes You Can Help
2017-09-28.abstract: Rain, wind and pollution have battered the cathedral, which is seeking funds in France and in the United States to help pay for extensive renovations.

2017-09-28.title: ISIS Releases Recording Said to Be of Its Leader
2017-09-28.abstract: The recording suggests that the Islamic State’s leader, Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, is still alive. It is his first public communication in nearly a year.

2017-09-28.title: Two North Korean Spies, a Ukrainian Jail and a Murky Tale
2017-09-28.abstract: Imprisoned for trying to steal missile technology, the two mostly keep to themselves but closely follow news about their nation’s nuclear weapons program.

2017-09-28.title: In Catalonia, an ‘Accidental’ Leader of Unwavering Conviction
2017-09-28.abstract: A compromise choice as Catalan leader, Carles Puigdemont is fully committed to an independence referendum in defiance of Spain’s central government

2017-09-29.title: Pressure Rises at U.N. on Myanmar Over Rohingya Crisis
2017-09-29.abstract: The leader of the United Nations called the Rohingya crisis “a human rights nightmare” as Myanmar canceled a U.N. visit to the affected area.

2017-09-29.title: Catalans and Kurds Discover the Hard Truth About Secession
2017-09-29.abstract: Independence referendums in Spain and Iraq reveal the deep contradiction between the norms and rules of secession.

2017-09-29.title: Cuban Doctors Revolt: ‘You Get Tired of Being a Slave’
2017-09-29.abstract: In a rare act of defiance, some 150 Cuban doctors serving in Brazil have sued to work as independent contractors, cutting out the Havana government.

2017-09-29.title: Fear of Volcano Eruption on Bali Drives 145,000 From Homes
2017-09-29.abstract: Mount Agung, which killed hundreds on the Indonesian island in the 1960s, has shown signs it will explode again soon. But no one can say exactly when.

2017-09-29.title: Helping the Rohingya
2017-09-29.abstract: A partial list of aid groups working to ease the humanitarian crisis in Myanmar and Bangladesh.

2017-09-30.title: After the Vote, Does the Kurdish Dream of Independence Have a Chance?
2017-09-30.abstract: Iraq’s Kurds voted overwhelmingly for an independent state, but their territory lacks democratic institutions, and their troubled economy is dependent on hostile neighbors.

2017-09-30.title: U.S. in Direct Communication With North Korea, Says Tillerson
2017-09-30.abstract: The confirmation marks the first time the Trump administration has acknowledged it is in contact with Pyongyang without the help of an intermediary.

2017-09-30.title: In Crimea, Russian Land Grab Feeds Cries of ‘Carpetbaggers!’
2017-09-30.abstract: In storied Sevastopol, government effort to confiscate land distributed free under Ukraine adds to long list of grievances over Russian rule.

2017-09-30.title: As Argentine Elections Approach, Two Disturbing Mysteries Loom
2017-09-30.abstract: A report says that a prosecutor investigating a 1994 bombing was murdered, and the disappearance of an activist stirs memories of the dictatorship.

2017-09-30.title: How Saudi Women Fought for the Right to Drive
2017-09-30.abstract: Women in Saudi Arabia have been fighting for the right to drive for 30 years. Four key milestone moments paved the way for the surprise overturning of the ban by the country's king. Meet the women behind the movement.
